title: Render ammeter transient current graphs in AnalogSimulationViewer
description: Fixes the issue where ammeter current waveforms were not rendered in the AnalogSimulationViewer despite being generated during simulation.
body: ## Summary `AnalogSimulationViewer` rendered simulation results by collecting only `simulation_transient_voltage_graph` IDs and forwarding them to `convertCircuitJsonToSchematicSimulationSvg`. Any `simulation_transient_current_graph` produced by an `` was silently dropped, so ammeter current waveforms never appeared in the viewer even though the simulation generated them. ## Changes - Collect `simulation_transient_current_graph_id`s from the circuit JSON alongside the existing voltage-graph IDs. - Pass `simulation_transient_current_graph_ids` through to `convertCircuitJsonToSchematicSimulationSvg` so current waveforms are rendered into the schematic simulation SVG. - Rename `simulationGraphIds` -> `simulationVoltageGraphIds` to make the voltage vs. current distinction explicit, and include both in the `useMemo` dependency arrays so the SVG regenerates when either changes. ## Reproduction Added `examples/example22-ammeter-current-probe.fixture.tsx`: a voltage source feeding a resistor through an ammeter (`AM1`), with a `VOUT` voltage probe. Before this change the ammeter's current graph was absent from the rendered simulation; after it, both the current and voltage waveforms render. ## Before / After | | Voltage graphs | Current graphs | |---|---|---| | Before | rendered | dropped | | After | rendered | rendered |

title: Preserve STEP face colors in GLB exports
description: Updates the STEP-to-GLB pipeline to preserve per-face colors from OCCT meshes in the exported model.
body:
This updates the STEP-to-GLB pipeline so per-face colors from OCCT meshes are preserved in the exported model.
test https://3d-viewer-ozg9r4jk4-tscircuit.vercel.app/?path=/story/bugs-kicad-tl3342-step-face-colors--step-only-local-fixture
## Changes

- extract STEP mesh -> Three.js group conversion into `step-mesh-to-group.ts`
- support `brep_faces` by creating geometry groups and per-face materials
- keep the mesh-level color as the default material when no face override exists
- update `GltfModel` hover highlighting to handle meshes with multiple materials
- bump the STEP GLB cache key so older cached single-material exports are invalidated

title: Fix duplicate same-net labels from schematic trace fallback insertion
description: Fixes the issue of duplicate overlapping schematic net labels for named nets by reusing existing labels instead of inserting duplicates.
body:
### Motivation

Named nets such as `BAT` could render duplicate overlapping schematic net labels near the same port. This happened in the fallback logic for ports missing a visible schematic trace: when a nearby label already represented the same source connection, the fallback did not safely reuse it for non-ground nets, so another label could be inserted for the same net.

In `repro132`, this showed up around `R8_BAT_TOP`, where `BAT` label could overlap or appear visually detached from the connected port.

### Change

- Reuse a nearby same-net schematic label instead of inserting a duplicate.
- Re-anchor an existing same-text label to the current schematic port so the label remains visually connected.
- Preserve explicit display labels by not renaming an existing nearby label when its text differs from the fallback label text.

Fixed repro132: https://github.com/tscircuit/core/pull/2501/changes#diff-08a18e894b09a4fc83026c2efefe3ba77a7be63ac6dcf90a0ce6fdffc8668efd

title: repro duplicate net label
description: Reproduces a bug related to duplicate net labels in a multidrop circuit configuration by adding a comprehensive test case.

title: Fix use array centers for QFN JSCAD geometry
description: Fixes the QFN footprint rendering issue by changing object-shaped centers to array-shaped centers for QFN body, pads, and thermal pad geometry.
body: ### Motivation QFN footprints failed in the vanilla JSCAD renderer used by 3d-viewer with: `Error: center must be an array of X, Y and Z values` This happened because QFN cuboids passed object-shaped centers instead of `[x, y, z]` arrays. ### Change - Use array centers for QFN body, pads, and thermal pad geometry. - Add a QFN20 fixture. - Add snapshot coverage for QFN20, QFN32, and QFN56 with thermal pad params. [REPRO](https://tscircuit.com/editor?#data:application/gzip;base64,)

title: Fix publish workflow auto-merge fallback
description: Fixes the auto-merge fallback in the publish workflow to handle cases where the pull request is already in a clean state, preventing stale version-bump PRs after a package is published.
body:
## Problem

The `Publish to npm` workflow could fail after the package was already published. The failing step was `Enable auto-merge`, where this command ran against the generated version-bump PR:

```bash
gh pr merge <pr> --auto --squash --delete-branch
```

GitHub sometimes creates that PR in a state where all required checks are already satisfied, so there is nothing for auto-merge to wait on. In real-life terms: the workflow was asking GitHub to "merge this later when it is ready", but GitHub was saying "it is ready right now, so there is no later auto-merge to enable."

The observed error was:

```text
GraphQL: Pull request Pull request is in clean status (enablePullRequestAutoMerge)
```

That left version-bump PRs open and stale even though the release itself had completed.

## Fix

The workflow still tries auto-merge first. If GitHub returns the known clean-status `enablePullRequestAutoMerge` error, it falls back to a direct squash merge. Other GitHub CLI errors still fail the job.

## Validation

- Parsed the workflow YAML.
- Checked the edited shell block with `bash -n`.
- Simulated the clean-status fallback and unrelated-error paths.
- Ran `bun run format`; it failed on existing `.vendor/rectdiff` Biome config incompatibilities and reported no fixes applied.

title: Repro right side vertical stack resistors
description: Reproduces a layout issue with right-side vertical stack resistors in the BQ24074 circuit configuration.

title: Repro resistors wrong placement
description: Fixes incorrect placement of resistors in the layout for the BQ24074 chip, ensuring they are positioned correctly under the chip in the schematic.

title: Add more snapshot tests
description: This pull request adds additional snapshot tests to improve the testing coverage of the project. The new tests include various input JSON files that represent different circuit configurations and their expected outputs. This enhancement aims to ensure that the circuit simulation behaves as expected across a wider range of scenarios.

title: Resolve copper pour connectivity by source net & usage documentation & cosmos deploy
description: Update the Circuit JSON adapter to use stable subcircuit_connectivity_map_key values for pour and pad connectivity, with source net idname lookup and validation against generated connectivity-map ids. Also expands README usage docs, adds a Cosmos docs siteexport setup for Vercel, and covers the new connectivity API behavior with tests.
body:
The updated snapshot is a fix not a regression

Update the Circuit JSON adapter to use stable subcircuit_connectivity_map_key values for pour and pad connectivity, with source net id/name lookup and validation against generated connectivity-map ids. Also expands README usage docs, adds a Cosmos docs site/export setup for Vercel, and covers the new connectivity API behavior with tests.

title: Accept anonymous fp_poly elements in KiCad S-expression parsing
description: Allows parsing of fp_poly elements in KiCad S-expressions without requiring uuid or tstamp attributes, enhancing flexibility in footprint definitions.

title: Update kicadts to accept fp_poly without uuid/tstamp
description: Updates the kicadts library to allow the conversion of fp_poly elements that do not include uuid or tstamp attributes, enhancing compatibility with certain KiCad footprint files.

title: Support simple_test_point inference for TP symbols and footprints
description: Classifies TP references and TestPoint library symbols as simple_test_point instead of simple_chip, extends symbol-library source component generation to emit simple_test_point, and adds PCB-side test point detection from reference prefixes and footprint metadata.
body:
### Summary

- classify `TP*` references and `TestPoint` / `TestPoint_Small` library symbols as `simple_test_point` instead of `simple_chip`
- extend symbol-library source component generation to emit `simple_test_point`
- reuse the shared symbol-library ftype inference in schematic symbol collection so TP symbols are handled consistently
- add PCB-side test point detection from both reference prefixes and footprint metadata
- add regression coverage for CM5IO symbol-library test points and Arduino Nano PCB test points
- refresh affected JSON/SVG/PNG snapshots to reflect the new classification and rendering

title: chore: bump version
description: Automated package update

title: Fill rectdiff gaps across multiple passes using maxGapFillPasses
description: Run gap filling across repeated findexpand passes until a pass adds no nodes, capping the loop at 4 total passes and updating SVG snapshots accordingly.
body:
## Summary

- Run gap filling across repeated find/expand passes until a pass adds no nodes.
- Cap the gap-fill loop at 4 total passes.
- Keep expanded-node visualization across all passes.
- Update SVG snapshots produced by the multi-pass gap fill.

## Root Cause

The previous gap fill only expanded once. Small corner-overlap gaps could require newly expanded nodes to participate in a later find/expand pass, leaving visible holes after the first pass.

Repro that got fixed
https://github.com/tscircuit/rectdiff/pull/133/changes#diff-5c564705806d916d0571b38e7be681977131bc6048582367372e594702516047

title: Add srj18 sample002 rectdiff visual fixture
description: Adds a standalone visual snapshot fixture for srj18 sample002. This PR intentionally contains only the fixture SRJ, the visual snapshot test, and the baseline SVG snapshot.
body: Adds a standalone visual snapshot fixture for srj18 sample002. This PR intentionally contains only the fixture SRJ, the visual snapshot test, and the baseline SVG snapshot.

title: Introduce Hosted 3D PCB Previews Generated Directly from Circuit JSON Jobs
description: Upgrades job previews from locally rendered PCB SVGs to hosted 3D PCB PNG previews generated from each jobs Circuit JSON.
body:
Upgrades job previews from locally rendered PCB SVGs to hosted 3D PCB PNG previews generated from each job’s Circuit JSON.

This PR includes:

* Circuit JSON to tscircuit JSX conversion for preview generation
* Hosted 3D PNG preview URLs via `createSvgUrl`
* Board-size extraction from Circuit JSON metadata
* Safe component-name generation from job names
* Updated preview loading and error states
* Fabrication page copy updated from SVG preview to 3D PNG preview
* Dependency updates for snippet URL generation and Circuit JSON conversion

This makes fabrication jobs easier to inspect visually by replacing flat SVG previews with richer generated 3D PCB renderings.

- add a dedicated `CC2340R52E0RKPR` chip component with the exact pin map, exposed pad, JLC part number, footprint, and CAD model metadata
- replace the previous generic `CC2340R5` definition with a part-specific implementation that matches the selected package and naming
